sourceMovie.title: The Man Who Knew Too Much
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Man Who Knew Too Much (1956) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Man Who Knew Too Much clicked for you, The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Edge of Darkness
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Man Who Knew Too Much clicked for you, Edge of Darkness (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Man Who Knew Too Much?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Ultimatum, Edge of Darkness, In the Heat of the Night—each shares DNA with The Man Who Knew Too Much while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Man Who Knew Too Much?
faq.answer: The Man Who Knew Too Much (1956) is best described as Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Man Who Knew Too Much worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
